MEMORANDUM OF CORRESPONDENCE

CORRESPONDENT [illegible]
DATED 4/9 RE: [illegible]

ADMINISTRATION
BOB T.
LINDA
JUDY

EXECUTIVE
DAN
MICKEY
CARLA
JEANNE
BOB S.

CONFLICTS
DEL
PN
KEN G.
ALFREDO

LEGAL
KEN T.
NATALIE
TED
MIKE

ENFORCEMENT
FRED
BARBARA
RICHARD

TAS
JOHN K.
GINA
MARIANN
DAWN
JAN

BERMA
JOHN G.
JAY
NAN-L.A.

DATE
REC'D. 4/13

INFORMATION

[illegible], please:

TAKE WHATEVER ACTION YOU CONSIDER APPROPRIATE
REPLY DIRECT BY _____ COPY TO [illegible]
PREPARE SUGGESTED REPLY AND RETURN TO _____
COORDINATE REPLY WITH _____

H1877 [?] from
Spoke & [?]on a
his Maj for influence a
call continuing by winner that
[illegible] Communistic spent &
fill magnanimies Govt the exert
be revoked be want to

DEAN AND DEAN
ATTORNEYS AT LAW
12892 MAIN STREET
POST OFFICE BOX 128
GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92642

KERN COUNTY: (714) 379-2332
ORANGE COUNTY: (714) 534-1860

April 4, 1977

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95814


Dear Sir:

This request for an opinion of the California Fair Political
Practice Commission is made by John R. Dean, Attorney at Law.

The facts are: Mr. John R. Dean contributed $4,549.49 to the
Kay Barr State Senate Campaign in the 1976, June Primary.
Mr. Dean also contributed $2,500.00 to Labor on the request of
Mr. Pete Remmel for expenses, because he had hired some help.
This contribution to Labor was later recorded by Mr. Remmel as
a contribution to C.O.P.E.. This money was not to a candidate
nor was a candidate or candidates ever discussed.

The question is: Is Mr. John R. Dean required to file a major
donor statement since only $4,549.49 was contributed to a
particular candidate, or is the contribution to Labor for ex-
penses considered a reportable contribution?

Respectfully submitted


JOHN R. DEAN

JRD:gk

sections.question: Is Mr. John R. Dean required to file a major donor statement since only $4,549.49 was contributed to a particular candidate, or is the contribution to Labor for expenses considered a reportable contribution?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether John R. Dean must file a major donor statement and whether a $2,500 contribution to Labor for expenses should be counted as a reportable contribution toward major donor status.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney on behalf of John R. Dean, not an FPPC response. The document also contains internal FPPC routing/distribution notation at the top indicating it was received on 4-13 and dated 4-4. The synthesis reflects the core legal questions about major donor status and contribution reportability.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Is Mr. John R. Dean required to file a major donor statement since only $4,549.49 was contributed to a particular candidate, or is the contribution to Labor for expenses considered a reportable contribution?
embedding.first_500_words: The document is a typewritten form with handwritten notes and annotations. Here's a summary of the content: **Header:** - **CORRESPONDENT:** John Smith, U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation - **DATE REC'D:** 4-13 - **DATED:** 4-4 - **RE:** [handwritten] Comparison **ADMINISTRATION:** - BOB T. - LINDA - JUDY **EXECUTIVE:** - DAN - MICKEY - CARLA - JEANNE - BOB S. **CONFLICTS:** - DEL - ED - KEN G. - ALFREDO **LEGAL:** - KEN P. - NATALIE - TED - MIKE **ENFORCEMENT:** - FRED - BARBARA - RICHARD **TA&R:** - JOHN K. - BERMA - GINA - JOHN G. - MARYANN - JAY - DAWN - NAN-L.A. - ALAN **INFORMATION:** - [handwritten] Linda, Please: - TAKE WHATEVER ACTION YOU CONSIDER APPROPRIATE - REPLY DIRECT BY [handwritten] 4-27 - COPY TO [handwritten] 2 CA - PREPARE SUGGESTED REPLY AND RETURN TO [handwritten] 2 CA - COORDINATE REPLY WITH [handwritten] 2 CA **Handwritten Note:** 4/18/77 I spoke to John Glenn and informed him that he became a major donor by virtue of the cost contribution committee, and that he would fill report. He indicated he would fill report to Sicilis, Kinchis. This document appears to be an internal communication within the Federal Bureau of Investigation, involving various staff members and their assignments or responsibilities. The handwritten note at the bottom provides additional context about a conversation with John Glenn regarding his donation and the subsequent report. April 4, 1977 State of California Fair Political Practices Commission Post Office Box 807 Sacramento, California 95814 Dear Sir: This request for an opinion of the California Fair Political Practice Commission is made by John R. Dean, Attorney at Law. The facts are: Mr. John R. Dean contributed $4,549.49 to the Kay Barr State Senate Campaign in the 1976, June Primary. Mr. Dean also contributed $2,500.00 to Labor on the request of Mr. Pete Remmel for expenses, because he had hired some help. This contribution to Labor was later recorded by Mr. Remmel as a contribution to C.O.P.E.. This money was not to a candidate nor was a candidate or candidates ever discussed. The question is: Is Mr. John R. Dean required to file a major donor statement since only $4,549.49 was contributed to a particular candidate, or is the contribution to Labor for expenses considered a reportable contribution? Respectfully submitted JOHN R. DEAN
embedding.summary: John R. Dean requests FPPC guidance on whether he qualifies as a major donor based on a $4,549.49 contribution to Kay Barr's 1976 State Senate campaign and whether a separate $2,500 Labor expense contribution is reportable.
